title: Hook for IntersectionObserver wrapping with cleanup and SSR safety
scenario_context: Track when element enters viewport (e.g., for animations, lazy loading, analytics). Hook wraps IntersectionObserver with SSR safety and proper cleanup.
responsive_reasoning: 1. IntersectionObserver is the right tool.\n2. Hook wraps for React patterns.\n3. Cleanup essential.
accessibility_across_viewports: Hidden content not perceived by screen readers via opacity.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Scroll-listener-based visibility is laggy.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Native IO. 2) Reusable hook. 3) Universal pattern.

title: Carousel item count varies by container width
scenario_context: Featured items carousel. Wide: shows 4 at a time. Narrow: 1 at a time. Container-driven.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Visible item count depends on container.\n2. Container queries on carousel.\n3. Snap aligned.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Same count everywhere breaks small containers.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Container-driven. 2) Snap. 3) Universal.

title: Variable font axes that respond to viewport via clamp
scenario_context: Variable font with weight axis (100-900). Heading weight scales with viewport — heavier on smaller for legibility, lighter on large for elegance.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Fixed font weight regardless of size
responsive_reasoning: 1. Variable fonts allow fluid axes.\n2. font-variation-settings via calc().\n3. CSS custom properties.
performance_notes: Variable font is one file for all weights.
edge_cases_handled: Narrow viewport — heavier weight for legibility
Static font fallback — uses set weight
what_breaks_naive_approach: Loading multiple weight files = slow. Static weight not optimal per size.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Variable font efficiency. 2) Size-appropriate weight. 3) Single file.

title: Negative space token system that scales asymmetrically
scenario_context: Design uses asymmetric whitespace — small vertical, large horizontal, etc. Each axis scales independently.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Each axis can have different curve.\n2. Tokens for each direction.\n3. Composable in components.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Uniform spacing forces same curve on both axes.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Per-axis curves. 2) Tokens. 3) Composition.

title: Stylus pressure-sensitive input handling for drawing
scenario_context: Drawing canvas. Apple Pencil, S Pen provide pressure data via PointerEvent. Use pressure for line thickness.
responsive_reasoning: 1. PointerEvent has pressure property.\n2. Map pressure to line thickness.\n3. Mouse pressure is 0 or 0.5 — defaults okay.
performance_notes: Direct canvas drawing. No React state thrash.
accessibility_across_viewports: Drawing is decorative; provide text alternative.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Same line thickness regardless of input feels artificial.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Pressure-sensitive. 2) PointerEvent. 3) Defaults for non-pressure.

title: Form field that scrolls to be perfectly centered when focused with keyboard up
scenario_context: When user taps an input, it should scroll to be visually centered in the available space (above keyboard).
responsive_reasoning: 1. visualViewport gives accurate visible space.\n2. Calculate desired position.\n3. Smooth scroll.

layout_strategy.content_priority: Focused field centered in visible area

edge_cases_handled: Keyboard appears — field centers in visible area
what_breaks_naive_approach: scrollIntoView({block: 'center'}) doesn't know about keyboard.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) visualViewport-aware. 2) Smooth centering. 3) Tolerance avoids tiny jitters.

title: Confetti animation that respects safe areas on iPhone
scenario_context: Success page shows confetti animation. Confetti should fall from top within visible safe area, not behind notch.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Confetti starts below notch.\n2. Falls to home indicator level.\n3. Safe area aware container.
performance_notes: CSS animations GPU. Reduced motion respected.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Confetti behind notch is invisible.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Safe area aware. 2) Reduced motion. 3) GPU animation.

title: Magazine-style article with image+text layout adapting to orientation
scenario_context: Magazine article with image accompanying text. Portrait: image above text. Landscape: image left, text wraps right.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Text below image always (wasted landscape space)
responsive_reasoning: 1. Landscape can have image alongside text.\n2. Portrait stacks naturally.\n3. shape-outside (advanced) for text wrap.

layout_strategy.primary_technique: Image float in landscape, stack in portrait

what_breaks_naive_approach: Stacked layout in landscape wastes horizontal.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Float for editorial wrap. 2) Stack in portrait. 3) Natural reading flow.

title: iPad-specific gesture: 3-finger swipe to undo, requires touch-action
scenario_context: iPad supports 3-finger swipe to undo. Web app can detect via TouchEvent count. Optional gesture.
responsive_reasoning: 1. 3-finger requires TouchEvent (not PointerEvent which is per-pointer).\n2. Always have undo button as alternative.\n3. iPad-specific feature.
accessibility_across_viewports: Undo button must exist as accessible alternative.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Required gesture-only undo locks out non-touch users.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) iPad-native gesture. 2) Always alternative. 3) Touch event proper handling.

title: Master-detail with deep linking that preserves nested selection state in URL
scenario_context: File browser. URL /folders/abc/files/xyz. Both folder and file selected — appropriate layout per viewport.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Nested selections in URL.\n2. Layout interprets URL.\n3. Universal URL works everywhere.

layout_strategy.primary_technique: URL-driven 3-pane on desktop, sequential on mobile

performance_notes: Each section can be a server component for individual data fetching.
accessibility_across_viewports: Back button has aria-label. Real navigation.
edge_cases_handled: Share link to file — opens at correct level
what_breaks_naive_approach: React state only loses on refresh. Different URLs per viewport are confusing.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) URL is universal state. 2) Layout interprets URL. 3) Deep links work everywhere.

title: Table that allows row dragging to reorder on touch and mouse
scenario_context: Reorderable table rows. Touch: long-press + drag. Mouse: drag handle. Keyboard: arrow keys when row focused.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Same as responsive-020 list pattern.\n2. Pointer events.\n3. Keyboard reorder mode.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Drag without long-press conflicts with scroll.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Touch + mouse + keyboard. 2) Long-press. 3) Universal pattern.

title: Tag input with autocomplete that suggests below input on desktop and dropdown on mobile
scenario_context: Tag input with suggestions. Desktop: dropdown below input. Mobile: full-screen sheet for space.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Mobile sheet has room.\n2. Desktop dropdown fits.\n3. Same suggestions.
performance_notes: Filter on each keystroke. Sheet only mounts when open mobile.
edge_cases_handled: Mobile sheet — full screen for browsing
what_breaks_naive_approach: Tiny dropdown on 320px viewport is hard to scroll.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Sheet on mobile. 2) Dropdown on desktop. 3) Same data.

title: Image comparison slider with touch and mouse drag
scenario_context: Two images stacked. Drag divider to reveal more of one or the other. Touch and mouse drag.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Pointer events unify.\n2. Position via percentage.\n3. Touch-friendly handle.
accessibility_across_viewports: role=slider with aria-valuenow. Keyboard arrow nav.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Mouse-only locks out touch.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Pointer events. 2) Keyboard support. 3) ARIA slider.

title: Persistent global notifications icon with badge count and adaptive dropdown
scenario_context: Bell icon shows unread count. Mobile: tap → full-screen sheet. Desktop: dropdown.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Mobile sheet, desktop dropdown.\n2. Badge with aria.\n3. Same data.
accessibility_across_viewports: aria-label on bell with count. role=dialog on sheet. ARIA live for new notifications (production).
what_breaks_naive_approach: Tiny dropdown on mobile is hard to use.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Sheet vs dropdown. 2) Badge with aria. 3) Same data.

title: Toast that becomes notification banner at top on mobile when persistent
scenario_context: Important system message (offline, update available). Should be visible until dismissed. Mobile: top banner. Desktop: corner toast that doesn't disappear.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Importance + viewport drives placement.\n2. Critical = top banner mobile.\n3. Non-critical = corner toast.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Auto-dismiss on critical messages loses important info.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Importance + viewport. 2) Banner vs toast. 3) Safe area.

title: Foldable device camera/preview app: app uses one screen, controls on other
scenario_context: Camera app on foldable in tabletop posture (laptop mode). Top half: viewfinder. Bottom half: controls.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Tabletop posture = vertical fold.\n2. Top segment for viewer.\n3. Bottom for controls.

layout_strategy.content_priority: Viewfinder on top, controls on bottom

edge_cases_handled: Flat unfolded — full viewfinder with controls overlay
what_breaks_naive_approach: Controls overlay on viewfinder when separate screen available wastes affordance.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Fold-aware layout. 2) Tabletop optimization. 3) Native API.

title: useDeviceCapabilities aggregator hook for SSR-safe device info
scenario_context: Many components need device info (touch, hover, motion, network). Aggregator hook provides one source.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Centralize device info.\n2. Context provider distributes.\n3. Single subscription set.
performance_notes: Single subscription set, distributed via context.
edge_cases_handled: Many components consume — single render path
what_breaks_naive_approach: Each component creating own matchMedia subscriptions = many listeners.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Centralized. 2) Context distribution. 3) Single source.

title: Subgrid for nested grid alignment within cards
scenario_context: Card grid where each card has title + body + footer that should align across cards. Subgrid achieves this.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Subgrid lets card cells participate in parent grid.\n2. Title/body/footer align across cards.\n3. Native CSS Grid feature.
edge_cases_handled: Body of different sizes — footer aligned
what_breaks_naive_approach: Cards with varied content have misaligned footers without subgrid.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Subgrid alignment. 2) Native CSS. 3) Visual consistency.

title: Optical sizing axis on variable fonts for large display vs body
scenario_context: Modern variable fonts (e.g., Roboto Flex) have 'opsz' axis. Larger sizes look better with optical axis adjusted.
responsive_reasoning: 1. opsz axis tunes per size.\n2. font-optical-sizing: auto handles.\n3. Manual override via font-variation-settings.
performance_notes: Single variable font handles all sizes optimally.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Same font tuning at all sizes — body looks too refined, headings too clunky.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Optical axis. 2) Auto adjustment. 3) Variable font advantage.

title: Section divider with adaptive style (line, gradient, spacing only) per density
scenario_context: Section breaks in article. Mobile: simple line. Wide: subtle gradient or pure spacing.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Divider style adapts to scale.\n2. Mobile: solid line. Desktop: spacing only.\n3. Container queries.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Same divider feels heavy on mobile, redundant on desktop.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Container-aware style. 2) Semantic hr. 3) Visual rhythm.

title: Hover-triggered actions with touch alternative via accessibility menu
scenario_context: Email list. Hover on item reveals quick actions (archive, delete). Touch users need alternative: tap-and-hold OR explicit menu button per row.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Hover for mouse, button for touch.\n2. Either always functional.\n3. Same actions.

layout_strategy.primary_technique: Hover reveal + always-visible menu button on touch

accessibility_across_viewports: Hover + focus-within for keyboard parity. Touch always has menu.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Hover-only locks out touch.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Hover for mouse. 2) Menu for touch. 3) Keyboard focus parity.

title: Form modal that locks scroll outside and prevents body bounce on iOS
scenario_context: Form modal. User scrolls inside. Body shouldn't scroll. iOS Safari bounce should be contained.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: overflow: hidden bug on iOS (body still scrolls)
responsive_reasoning: 1. iOS body scroll lock is tricky.\n2. overscroll-behavior: contain on modal.\n3. Body fix via position: fixed + top: -scrollY.

detection_strategy.code_pattern: On open: store scrollY, body.style.position = 'fixed', body.style.top = `-${scrollY}px`

edge_cases_handled: Multiple modals stack — only outermost lock active
what_breaks_naive_approach: overflow: hidden on body fails iOS rubber-band.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) iOS-specific fix. 2) Scroll restore. 3) Universal.

title: Split-screen layout that respects safe area on devices that have them
viewport_spectrum: iPad with home indicator, iPhone in landscape
scenario_context: App with split-screen UI. Both halves must respect their edges of safe areas. Left half: notch on left in landscape. Right half: right safe area.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Each half handles its edge insets.\n2. Center has no inset.\n3. CSS env() per direction.
edge_cases_handled: Landscape with notch on one side — that side respects
No safe area device — env returns 0
what_breaks_naive_approach: Center-only or full padding loses edge specificity.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Per-pane insets. 2) Center has none. 3) Edge-specific handling.

title: Calendar that shows week or day view based on orientation
scenario_context: Calendar app. Portrait: day view (single column). Landscape: week view (7 columns).
responsive_reasoning: 1. Orientation drives view choice.\n2. Same data; presentation varies.\n3. State preserved.

interaction_model_per_device.mobile_touch: Portrait: scroll day. Landscape: scroll week.

what_breaks_naive_approach: 7-column on phone portrait unreadable.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Orientation drives view. 2) Same events. 3) CSS-only switch.

title: Picture-in-picture support for tablet multitasking
scenario_context: Video that supports browser PIP. iPad can use PIP while another app open. Provide PIP button.
responsive_reasoning: 1. PIP browser API available.\n2. Show button only when supported.\n3. Native browser behavior.
accessibility_across_viewports: aria-label on button. Hidden when unsupported.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Hardcoded PIP button errors on unsupported browsers.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Feature detection. 2) Native API. 3) Optional enhancement.

title: Floating action button transforms into bottom sheet trigger on mobile
scenario_context: FAB on desktop opens dropdown menu. On mobile, taps open bottom sheet with options.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Same trigger, different overlay.\n2. Sheet for thumb. Dropdown for cursor.\n3. Same actions.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Same overlay everywhere is wrong size for one viewport.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Adaptive overlay. 2) Reusable pattern. 3) Safe area.

title: Pagination + virtual scrolling combined for huge tables
scenario_context: 100,000 row table. Combine pagination (server-side) with virtual scrolling (client-side) for performance.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Server pagination limits payload.\n2. Virtual scroll within page.\n3. Both layers.
accessibility_across_viewports: Pagination accessible. Virtual scroll preserves keyboard nav.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Loading 100k rows kills browser.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Server + client optimization. 2) Scales massively. 3) Universal pattern.

title: Camera capture form field with preview and retake option
scenario_context: Form needs photo capture. Mobile camera direct capture with preview + retake.
responsive_reasoning: 1. capture attribute enables direct camera.\n2. Preview from File via URL.createObjectURL.\n3. Retake clears.

interaction_model_per_device.mobile_touch: Tap → camera. Preview shown. Retake button.
interaction_model_per_device.desktop_mouse: Webcam if supported, otherwise file picker

accessibility_across_viewports: Real label for input. alt on preview.
edge_cases_handled: Desktop fallback to file picker (no capture)
what_breaks_naive_approach: No preview = user uncertain. No retake = stuck with first photo.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) capture attribute. 2) Preview + retake. 3) Native fallback.

title: SVG icon system with adaptive size via currentColor and em sizing
scenario_context: Icon system. Icons should: inherit color from parent (currentColor), scale with font size (em units), be crisp at any DPR.
responsive_reasoning: 1. SVG inherits currentColor.\n2. width: 1em scales with text.\n3. Crisp at any DPR (vector).
edge_cases_handled: Scales with text size via em
what_breaks_naive_approach: Hardcoded fill doesn't theme. Fixed px doesn't scale with text.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) currentColor. 2) em sizing. 3) Vector crispness.

title: Account menu with avatar that expands to user info dropdown
scenario_context: User avatar in header. Click reveals dropdown with name, settings link, sign-out. Adapts to viewport.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Avatar serves as trigger.\n2. Dropdown/sheet adapts.\n3. Per responsive-045 pattern.
edge_cases_handled: Mobile sheet with full user info
Logged out — sign in button instead
what_breaks_naive_approach: Same dropdown on mobile is cramped.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Adaptive menu. 2) Avatar trigger. 3) Composition.
